fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Wisconsin Certified Dietitian (CD) Application — Requirements & Fees (2026)
seo.description: Apply for Wisconsin Certified Dietitian credential through DSPS. $60 application fee. CDR exam required. 900 hours supervised practice with clinical, food service, and community components.
seo.keywords: Wisconsin dietitian license, WI certified dietitian requirements, Wisconsin DSPS dietitian, Wisconsin dietitian application fee

board.name: Dietitians Affiliated Credentialing Board
board.parent_dept: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)

license_types.title: Certified Dietitian (CD)
license_types.description: Wisconsin's credential for practicing dietitians, issued by the Dietitians Affiliated Credentialing Board under DSPS. The state is considering transitioning to 'Licensed Dietitian Nutritionist (LDN)' under pending legislation.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a bachelor's degree or higher from a regionally accredited institution in dietetics, human nutrition, or a related field. Complete at least 900 hours of supervised practice including 300 hours in clinical dietetics, 120 hours in food service administration, and 120 hours in community nutrition.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the registration examination for dietitians established by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR), or an equivalent examination approved by the Wisconsin Dietitians Affiliated Credentialing Board.

steps.title: Submit the Online Application
steps.description: Apply online through the DSPS LicensE portal. Pay the $60 credential fee. Upload proof of CDR exam passage or current CDR registration verification.

steps.title: Provide Education & Practice Documentation
steps.description: Submit satisfactory evidence of your qualifying education and completion of the supervised practice requirements, including official transcripts and internship verification.

documents.detail: From a regionally accredited institution, showing degree in an approved field.

documents.item: CDR Registration Verification or Exam Results
documents.detail: Proof of passing the CDR registration examination or current CDR credential.
Verification of 900+ hours, including breakdown of clinical (300 hrs), food service (120 hrs), and community nutrition (120 hrs) components.

provisional_text: Wisconsin does not currently offer a provisional or temporary dietitian credential. Applicants must meet all requirements, including passing the CDR exam, before certification is issued.
reciprocity_text: Wisconsin does not offer formal licensure by reciprocity. Out-of-state dietitians must apply through the standard process via the DSPS LicensE portal. Holding current CDR registration typically satisfies the education and examination requirements. Wisconsin's supervised practice hour requirements (900 hours with specific clinical/food service/community breakdowns) may differ from other states.

faqs.question: How much does a Wisconsin dietitian certification cost?
faqs.answer: The initial credential fee is $60. Renewal is also $60, due by October 31 of even-numbered years. Late renewal after five years incurs an additional $25 fee.

faqs.question: What are Wisconsin's supervised practice hour requirements?
faqs.answer: Wisconsin requires a minimum of 900 hours of supervised dietetics practice, including at least 300 hours in clinical dietetics, 120 hours in food service administration, and 120 hours in community nutrition — all under approved supervision.

faqs.question: Is Wisconsin transitioning to licensure?
faqs.answer: Yes. There are active legislative discussions about transitioning from 'certification' to 'licensure,' which would change the credential to Licensed Dietitian Nutritionist (LDN) and potentially update fees. Monitor the DSPS website for updates.
